description: A scheduling request — draft should propose a concrete time or commit to one of the offered slots.

email.subject: Quick call next week?
email.snippet: Could we grab 30 minutes next week to catch up on the integration work?
email.body:
Hey,

Hope you're doing well. Could we grab 30 minutes next week to catch up on the integration work? Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon would be great on my end.

Frank

rubric:
Score this draft on a 10-point scale by summing the criteria below. Do not invent additional criteria.

## Criteria (sum these — total caps at 10)

**TIME COMMITMENT (4 points)**
- +4 if the draft picks ONE specific slot from the offered options (Tuesday afternoon or Wednesday afternoon) OR proposes a specific alternative time/day
- +2 if it asks Frank to pick which of the two offered slots works for him (acceptable but lazier)
- +1 if it just says 'either works' or echoes both options back
- 0 if it offers no time commitment at all

**DURATION ACKNOWLEDGED (2 points)**
- +2 if the draft confirms the 30-minute duration
- 0 if it doesn't mention duration

**INTEGRATION-WORK CONTEXT (2 points)**
- +2 if the draft mentions the integration work or otherwise shows it understood the topic
- 0 if generic ('happy to chat', 'looking forward to catching up') with no topic-specific content

**TONE (2 points)**
- +2 if conversational, appropriate for an existing customer relationship
- +1 if slightly stiff but acceptable
- 0 if robotic or over-formal

## Penalties (subtract from total)

- -2 if the draft proposes a specific time OUTSIDE the offered Tue/Wed afternoon window WITHOUT acknowledging the original options
- -2 if it defers to a scheduling assistant (this fixture has EA disabled)
- -3 if the draft fabricates calendar availability claims

## Output

Return the clamped integer total (0-10). The reason must enumerate which criteria you scored above 0.
